root_text.an4.102:1.8: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cattāro valāhakūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.102:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo gajjitā hoti, no vassitā?
root_text.an4.102:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.102:2.3: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.102:2.4: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.102:2.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo gajjitā hoti, no vassitā.
root_text.an4.102:2.6: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, valāhako gajjitā, no vassitā;
root_text.an4.102:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo vassitā hoti, no gajjitā?
root_text.an4.102:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo dhammaṁ na pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.102:3.3: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.102:3.4: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.102:3.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo vassitā hoti, no gajjitā.
root_text.an4.102:3.6: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, valāhako vassitā, no gajjitā;
root_text.an4.102:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo neva gajjitā hoti, no vassitā?
root_text.an4.102:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo neva dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.102:4.3: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.102:4.4: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.102:4.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo neva gajjitā hoti, no vassitā.
root_text.an4.102:4.6: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, valāhako neva gajjitā, no vassitā;
root_text.an4.102:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo gajjitā ca hoti vassitā ca?
root_text.an4.102:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.102:5.3: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.102:5.4: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.102:5.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo gajjitā ca hoti vassitā ca.
root_text.an4.102:5.6: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, valāhako gajjitā ca vassitā ca;
root_text.an4.102:5.8: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro valāhakūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.102:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of clouds.
translation_text.an4.102:1.5: one neither thunders nor rains, and
translation_text.an4.102:1.7: These are the four kinds of clouds.
translation_text.an4.102:1.8: In the same way, these four individuals similar to clouds are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.102:1.12: one neither thunders nor rains, and
translation_text.an4.102:2.1: And how does an individual thunder but not rain?
translation_text.an4.102:2.2: It’s when an individual memorizes the teaching—
translation_text.an4.102:2.3: statements, mixed prose and verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an4.102:2.4: But they don’t truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.an4.102:2.5: That’s how an individual thunders but doesn’t rain.
translation_text.an4.102:2.6: That individual is like a cloud that thunders but doesn’t rain, I say.
translation_text.an4.102:3.1: And how does an individual rain but not thunder?
translation_text.an4.102:3.2: It’s when an individual doesn’t memorize the teaching …
translation_text.an4.102:3.4: But they truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.102:4.1: And how does an individual neither thunder nor rain?
translation_text.an4.102:4.2: It’s when an individual doesn’t memorize the teaching …
translation_text.an4.102:4.4: Nor do they truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.102:5.1: And how does an individual both thunder and rain?
translation_text.an4.102:5.2: It’s when an individual memorizes the teaching …
translation_text.an4.102:5.4: And they truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.102:5.8: These four individuals similar to clouds are found in the world.”
